breaks: AT-028.07/08's withholding of assistance exceeds REQ-034's explicit ceiling of "detection and suggestion only, never gating"; making the gate Skill-local does not remove that contradiction.
"Binding floors to unattributed (a floor, not a bypass)" describes task-system-auth degradation, not authority for Skill refusal or opt-out behavior.

Disabling may be unrestricted as a Skill opt-out, but it cannot bypass REQ-009 fuel, project-binding, revocation/AUP, or provider controls. A raw request is guaranteed only not to be rejected because attribution is missing.
tensions.amended_resolution: A founder must choose whether the default Skill may pause its own guided assistance. If layered enforcement is chosen, the gateway/platform must still forward every otherwise-valid metered request regardless of attribution, recording missing bindings as unattributed; the Skill may pause only its optional local workflow until task-or-exploration selection. Disable must be immediate, persistent, approval-free, and penalty-free, while unrelated gateway controls remain effective.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: Replace REQ-034's steering ceiling with: "Attribution telemetry and platform/gateway steering are detection-and-suggestion only and never gate forwarding of an otherwise-valid metered request. The optional local Skill may pause only its own guided workflow pending task-or-exploration selection; disabling it is immediate and degrades attribution to unattributed." Clarify REQ-028's floor as the auth-failure attribution floor, not a metering or security bypass.

breaks: The proposed branch-link mirror is not Linear's actual invariant. Copying a branch name may optionally move an issue to a started status, while opening a linked PR defaults to In Progress; these are configurable, independent automations, not confirmation of prior self-assignment. See [Linear's GitHub documentation](https://linear.app/docs/github) and [Code & Reviews documentation](https://linear.app/docs/code-and-reviews).

Changing only REQ-026's acceptance criterion leaves conflicting text in REQ-026's Model and Write-authority paragraphs, REQ-028's branch-link clause, REQ-008's status-provenance clause, and Technical Considerations.

A branch or PR carrying the wrong task ID can mutate the wrong Linear issue. The current detect-and-revert rule would incorrectly accept that Done transition because a linked merge exists, unless "verified" also requires correspondence with the explicit pull, project, assignee, and worktree/task mapping.

AT-026.10 remains valid; AT-026.17 needs unmatched/wrong-ID negatives; AT-028.17 must stop asserting that a branch link independently marks In Progress; AT-008.13 and AT-012.05 must use the strengthened verified-merge definition.
tensions.amended_resolution: Explicit volunteer- or Skill-confirmed self-assignment is the sole authority for entering In Progress; platform automation projects that event into Linear. Native branch-copy and PR-open status automation must not be treated as authority. A branch/PR link is corroborating linkage only. If it fires without a prior explicit pull, queued/unassigned state wins and any vendor-created assignment/status is reverted and flagged; a later explicit pull may then enter In Progress. Done requires a verified merge whose task ID matches the project, PR author/assignee, and recorded pull/worktree mapping. A wrong-ID link advances no task and triggers detect-and-revert.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: In REQ-026 Model, Write authority, and AC, state: "Explicit self-assignment authorizes In Progress; platform automation mirrors it. Only a matching verified PR merge authorizes Done; all other status changes are reverted." In REQ-028 replace "a branch link marks In Progress" with "a branch/PR link corroborates the pulled task but does not independently change status." Apply the same two-authority summary to Technical Considerations.

breaks: No direct REQ-010 break: the project page and progress already derive exclusively from Linear and omit GitHub Issues.

REQ-012 remains consistent only if every bug, refactor, or debt item required for current P0 acceptance is represented as a P0 Linear task; otherwise an open completion-critical GitHub Issue would still not block completion.

"Dev-internal" cannot mean confidential or literally invisible to an NGO because the repository is public and the NGO may open Issues post-completion; it means outside ai4good's NGO-facing PM surfaces and lifecycle authority.
tensions.amended_resolution: REQ-008's list governs: GitHub Issues may hold optional dev-internal code work—bugs, refactors, and technical debt—but never serve as the source of truth for a current deliverable, scope item, progress, or completion. Any such item that becomes acceptance-critical must exist as a P0 Linear task; a GitHub Issue may be cross-linked but remains non-authoritative. Post-completion NGO-created Issues remain allowed and do not reopen the completed project.
tensions.minimal_prd_change: Replace REQ-026's "GitHub Issues (code bugs only)" with "GitHub Issues (dev-internal code work: bugs, refactors, and technical debt; never deliverable or scope authority). Any item required for P0 acceptance must be represented in Linear."

overall: T3 is editorial harmonization. T2 has the correct intended authority but needs a vendor-accurate event and reconciliation rule across every conflicting occurrence. T1 cannot be accepted as a no-change clarification: Skill refusal conflicts with REQ-034's exact ceiling and requires an explicit founder choice plus a mandatory layer-boundary edit.
